text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY CONSULTATION NOTE

Patient: Denise Okafor
Attending: [Medical Oncologist]
Department: Medical Oncology
Visit Type: New Patient Consultation
Referring Provider: Breast Surgery / Multidisciplinary Tumor Board

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Ms. Okafor is a 49-year-old woman of West African descent referred for medical oncology consultation regarding newly diagnosed left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, triple-negative subtype, Stage IIB (T2N1M0). She presents today following completion of staging workup and multidisciplinary tumor board (MTB) review.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Okafor is a 49-year-old woman, otherwise healthy, who presented to her primary care physician approximately 3 weeks ago after self-detecting a firm, non-tender mass in her left breast upper outer quadrant. She denies any prior breast complaints, nipple discharge, skin changes, or prior abnormal mammograms. She underwent diagnostic workup including diagnostic mammogram and bilateral breast MRI, which demonstrated a 3.2 x 2.8 cm irregular spiculated enhancing mass in the left breast upper outer quadrant with associated skin thickening and left axillary adenopathy (two nodes with cortical thickening up to 8 mm). BIRADS 5. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed separately showed no visceral or osseous metastases; left axillary lymphadenopathy consistent with known nodal disease.

Ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy of the left breast mass was performed and pathology confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma, poorly differentiated (Nottingham grade 3/3), with Ki-67 85%. Receptor profile: ER negative (Allred 0/8), PR negative (Allred 0/8), HER2 1+ by IHC with non-amplified FISH (ratio 1.4) — confirming triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC). Stromal TILs estimated at 35%. Sentinel lymph node biopsy performed at the time of diagnostic biopsy: 1 of 3 nodes positive for metastatic carcinoma, largest deposit 4 mm, no extranodal extension.

PD-L1 CPS was assessed by 22C3 assay and returned at 18 (positive).

Next-generation sequencing (tumor/normal paired, comprehensive panel) was performed. Germline testing identified a pathogenic BRCA2 variant: c.5946delT (p.S1982Rfs*22). Somatic alterations include TP53 p.R248W (VAF 42%), PIK3CA p.H1047R (VAF 18%), RB1 splice-site loss (VAF 31%), PTEN p.R130Q (VAF 24%), MYC amplification (copy number 12), CDKN2A homozygous deletion, and CCNE1 amplification (copy number 6). TMB 11 mut/Mb (intermediate). MSS. HRD score 68 (positive, threshold >42). Genomic LOH 22%.

The case was presented at our multidisciplinary tumor board this week. The consensus recommendation is for neoadjuvant systemic therapy followed by definitive surgery. I am seeing Ms. Okafor today to discuss the diagnosis, the genomic findings, and the recommended treatment plan in detail, and to address her questions and concerns.

She reports feeling shocked but motivated. She denies current dyspnea, bone pain, headache, or neurologic symptoms. She reports no constitutional symptoms beyond mild anxiety. She is currently on no oncologic therapy.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Positive: Palpable left breast mass (presenting complaint), mild anxiety. Denies fevers, chills, unintentional weight loss, night sweats, bone pain, shortness of breath, hemoptysis, abdominal pain, jaundice, or neurologic symptoms. Menstrual cycles regular. No nipple discharge. No skin rash.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
1. No prior malignancy
2. No prior breast biopsies
3. No significant cardiac, pulmonary, hepatic, or renal disease
4. No known thyroid disease
5. No prior surgeries except tonsillectomy in childhood

---

FAMILY HISTORY
- Mother: Ovarian cancer, diagnosed at age 52 (deceased)
- Maternal aunt: Breast cancer, diagnosed at age 44
- No paternal history of breast, ovarian, or other hereditary cancers known
- Strong maternal family history consistent with BRCA-associated hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome

---

SOCIAL HISTORY
Ms. Okafor is a high school biology teacher; she lives with her husband and two adult children. She is a lifelong non-smoker. Denies alcohol use beyond social (less than 2 drinks per week). No recreational drug use. BMI 27.4 kg/m2. Physically active (walks 30 minutes most days). Born in Nigeria, immigrated to the United States as a young adult. She has strong family and community support.

---

ALLERGIES
No known drug allergies. No known food allergies.

---

MEDICATIONS
1. Ibuprofen 400 mg PRN (occasional, not in recent weeks per patient report)
2. Multivitamin daily
No chronic prescription medications.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vital Signs: BP 118/74 mmHg, HR 76 bpm, RR 14, Temp 37.0°C, SpO2 99% on room air, Weight 71 kg, Height 161 cm, BSA 1.73 m2
ECOG Performance Status: 0

General: Alert, well-appearing woman in no acute distress. Appropriate affect. Engaged and asks thoughtful questions.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Sclerae non-icteric. Oropharynx clear.
Neck/Lymph Nodes: No palpable cervical or supraclavicular adenopathy.
Chest/Breast: Right breast without palpable mass, skin change, or nipple discharge. Left breast: firm, irregular, non-tender mass palpable in the upper outer quadrant, approximately 3 cm clinically. Overlying skin with subtle focal thickening but no frank erythema, peau d'orange, or ulceration. Left nipple without retraction or discharge.
Axillae: Left axilla: one clinically palpable, firm, mildly enlarged lymph node, approximately 1.5 cm; non-tender. Right axilla: no palpable lymphadenopathy.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs.
Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly or splenomegaly.
Extremities: No edema. No calf tenderness.
Neurologic: Alert and oriented x3. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. No focal deficits.
Skin: No rash, petechiae, or pigmented lesions of concern.

---

LABORATORY AND DIAGNOSTIC DATA
CBC (obtained at referring office, recently): WBC 6.2 x10^9/L, Hgb 12.4 g/dL, Plt 234 x10^9/L — within normal limits.
CMP: Na 139, K 4.0, BUN 14, Cr 0.82, glucose 91. Total bilirubin 0.6, direct bilirubin 0.2, ALT 22 U/L, AST 19 U/L, alkaline phosphatase 58 U/L — all within normal limits.
TSH: 1.9 mIU/L (normal).
Echo: Not yet obtained; to be ordered prior to anthracycline initiation.
Germline BRCA2: Pathogenic variant c.5946delT (p.S1982Rfs*22) confirmed.

Biopsy Pathology (left breast core needle):
- Invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 3/3 (tubule 3, pleomorphism 3, mitoses 3; >20 mitoses/10 HPF)
- ER negative, PR negative, HER2 1+ (IHC; FISH ratio 1.4, non-amplified) — confirmed triple-negative
- Ki-67: 85%
- TILs: 35% stromal
- PD-L1 CPS (22C3): 18
- Sentinel lymph node: 1/3 positive, 4 mm deposit, no extranodal extension

NGS Key Findings:
- Germline BRCA2 c.5946delT (pathogenic)
- Somatic: TP53 R248W, PIK3CA H1047R, RB1 splice-site loss, PTEN R130Q, MYC amplification (CN 12), CDKN2A homozygous deletion, CCNE1 amplification (CN 6)
- HRD score 68 (positive); gLOH 22%; TMB 11 mut/Mb; MSS

Imaging Summary:
- Breast MRI: 3.2 x 2.8 cm spiculated enhancing mass, left upper outer quadrant; type III kinetics; left axillary adenopathy (2 nodes, up to 8 mm cortical thickening). No satellite lesions. Right breast normal.
- CT CAP (staging): No pulmonary nodules, hepatic lesions, or osseous metastases. Left axillary adenopathy present, consistent with known disease. No mediastinal or supraclavicular nodes. Impression: Stage IIB, no distant metastases.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Ms. Okafor is a 49-year-old woman with newly diagnosed Stage IIB (T2N1M0, AJCC 8th ed.) triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma, left breast, with the following high-risk features: grade 3, Ki-67 85%, lymphovascular invasion on biopsy, positive sentinel node, TILs 35%, PD-L1 CPS 18 (positive), germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant, and HRD-positive tumor biology (HRD score 68, gLOH 22%). Her staging workup does not demonstrate distant metastatic disease.

This case was reviewed at multidisciplinary tumor board; the following management plan was formulated and discussed at length with the patient today.

1. NEOADJUVANT SYSTEMIC THERAPY — KEYNOTE-522 REGIMEN:
   Ms. Okafor is an excellent candidate for neoadjuvant pembro-based therapy per the KEYNOTE-522 schema. Rationale includes: PD-L1 CPS 18, TILs 35%, high-grade/high-proliferation TNBC, and resectable Stage IIB disease where pCR is the goal. The regimen will consist of:
   - Keytruda (Keytruda) 200 mg IV Q3W x 4 cycles concurrent with dose-dense doxo (Adriamycin) 60 mg/m2 + cyclo 600 mg/m2 Q2W x 4 cycles (ddAC). G-CSF support with pegfilgrastim (Neulasta) 6 mg SC on Day 2 of each ddAC cycle.
   - Followed by Keytruda 200 mg IV Q3W concurrent with weekly PTX (Taxol) 80 mg/m2 IV x 12 weeks.
   Premedications per protocol (ondansetron, dexamethasone, lorazepam). LVEF assessment (echocardiogram) to be completed prior to first anthracycline dose.
   I explained the rationale for combining immunotherapy with chemotherapy in this setting, citing the KEYNOTE-522 data demonstrating improved pathologic complete response rates and event-free survival benefit.

2. GERMLINE BRCA2 FINDINGS AND GENETIC COUNSELING:
   Pathogenic BRCA2 variant (c.5946delT) identified on tumor/normal paired NGS, confirmed in peripheral blood. I have placed a referral to genetic counseling for formal counseling, cascade testing discussion for first-degree relatives, and enrollment in the BRCA2 carrier registry. I discussed with Ms. Okafor that this result has implications beyond her current breast cancer diagnosis — including lifetime risk of ovarian cancer, contralateral breast cancer, and implications for her biological children. She expressed understanding and plans to inform her family members.
   - Lynparza (Lynparza): Given germline BRCA2 status and high-risk disease features, adjuvant ola per the OlympiA protocol is a potential future option following completion of neoadjuvant and surgical therapy. This was introduced conceptually today; definitive decision will be made based on surgical pathology response.

3. SURGICAL PLANNING:
   Definitive surgery will be planned after completion of neoadjuvant therapy. Options to be discussed with breast surgery team include left breast-conserving surgery vs. mastectomy. Given germline BRCA2 status, prophylactic surgery on the contralateral breast will also be an important discussion. She will be seen jointly with breast surgery.

4. POST-NEOADJUVANT STRATEGY:
   We discussed that the pathologic response at surgery will guide adjuvant therapy decisions. If residual disease is present, options include cape (Xeloda) per CREATE-X protocol, with continued Keytruda to complete one year of total immunotherapy. If pCR is achieved, Keytruda will be continued to complete one year per protocol.

5. MONITORING AND TOXICITY COUNSELING:
   Patient counseled on the following potential toxicities:
   - Immune-related adverse events (irAEs) with pembro: thyroiditis, pneumonitis, hepatitis, colitis, adrenal insufficiency — patient instructed on symptoms to report immediately. She was given written materials.
   - Anthracycline-related cardiotoxicity: LVEF monitoring planned.
   - Taxane-associated peripheral neuropathy: will monitor for dose modification.
   - Alopecia: anticipated with anthracycline and taxane; wigs/cold cap discussed.
   - Fertility: Ms. Okafor's family is complete; chemotherapy-induced amenorrhea discussed.
   - Bone marrow suppression: CBC monitoring schedule reviewed; pegfilgrastim for neutropenia prophylaxis.

6. FERTILITY/MENSTRUAL CONSIDERATIONS:
   Patient reports her family is complete. Chemotherapy-related ovarian suppression discussed. Given germline BRCA2 status, risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (RRBSO) will be considered after oncologic treatment is complete; referral to gynecologic oncology to be placed at appropriate time.

7. SUPPORTIVE CARE:
   Nutrition consultation placed. Social work referral offered and accepted (patient expressed interest in support group resources). Integrative oncology consultation available if desired.

8. RETURN VISIT:
   Patient will return in approximately one week for pre-treatment baseline labs (CBC, CMP, LFTs, TSH) and echocardiogram results review, and to initiate Cycle 1 of Keytruda + ddAC. Signed consent obtained today for all planned systemic agents.

Total time with patient: approximately 75 minutes. Questions answered thoroughly. Patient verbalized understanding and is ready to proceed.

Electronically signed,
[Attending Medical Oncologist]
Medical Oncology
